title: Fix screenshot analysis: simplify Gemini prompt for basic next-candle prediction

requirements.summary: Rewrite Gemini API prompt to be minimal and focused on next-candle prediction
acceptanceCriteria: The prompt text in analysisApi.ts is under 200 words
The prompt explicitly requests a JSON-only response with no markdown or code fences
The prompt asks Gemini to identify the candlestick chart area within the full screenshot
The prompt asks for next-candle direction prediction based on the last 5 candles
The prompt requests exactly these fields: sinal, tendencia, confianca, padroes, explicacao
No complex multi-step scoring system or broker-specific detection logic is present in the prompt

file_operations.description: Replace the existing Gemini API prompt text with a new minimal, direct instruction (under 200 words) that: (1) tells Gemini to locate the candlestick chart area in the image and ignore other screen elements; (2) instructs Gemini to examine the last 5 visible candles and overall trend; (3) asks for a prediction of whether the NEXT candle is likely bullish or bearish; (4) explicitly requests a raw JSON response containing only these fields: sinal ('COMPRA'/'VENDA'/'SEM ENTRADA'), tendencia ('ALTA'/'BAIXA'/'LATERAL'), confianca (0-100 number), padroes (array of max 3 short Portuguese pattern names), explicacao (one Portuguese sentence explaining the prediction); (5) explicitly states that Gemini must return raw JSON only with no markdown code blocks or extra text. Remove any references to broker detection, complex scoring systems, or multi-step analysis workflows from the prompt.

requirements.summary: Update Gemini API response parsing to robustly extract JSON from wrapped or mixed responses
acceptanceCriteria: Parser strips markdown code fences (```json and ```) before attempting JSON.parse
Parser falls back to extracting the first { } block if direct parse fails
Raw Gemini response text is logged to the browser console on every request
An AnalysisApiError with Portuguese message is thrown when JSON cannot be extracted
Valid JSON responses parse correctly without modification

file_operations.description: Update the Gemini API response parsing logic in the analyzeChartImage function to: (1) log the raw response text to the browser console for debugging on every request; (2) strip markdown code fence wrappers (```json ... ``` or ``` ... ```) from the response text before attempting JSON.parse; (3) if direct JSON.parse fails, attempt to extract the first { ... } block from the response text using a regex pattern and parse that; (4) throw an AnalysisApiError with the Portuguese message 'Resposta inválida da IA — tente novamente' if no valid JSON can be extracted after all fallback attempts; (5) ensure that valid JSON responses continue to parse correctly without modification or data loss.

requirements.summary: Ensure base64 image data is sent without data URI prefix and with correct MIME type
acceptanceCriteria: The data URI prefix 'data:[mimetype];base64,' is stripped before placing base64 data in inlineData.data
The mimeType value reflects the actual uploaded file's type
The base64 string sent to Gemini is raw (no prefix)
A console.log confirms the mimeType and base64 length before the fetch call

file_operations.description: Update the image-to-base64 conversion and Gemini API request payload construction to: (1) after converting the file to a base64 data URI, strip the 'data:[mimetype];base64,' prefix from the string before placing it in the inlineData.data field; (2) extract the actual file MIME type (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'image/png', 'image/webp') from the file object and set it in the mimeType field (never hardcode a fixed value); (3) add a console.log statement before the fetch call that outputs the mimeType and the length of the raw base64 string to confirm the correct format; (4) ensure the Gemini Vision API receives raw base64 data in inlineData.data without any data URI prefix.
